name: Lee Harvey Oswald Patsy
description: The "Lee Harvey Oswald Patsy" theory posits that Lee Harvey Oswald, officially identified as the assassin of President John F. Kennedy, was not the sole perpetrator but rather a scapegoat set up to take the blame for a larger conspiracy. Proponents argue that Oswald was a pawn used by powerful forces, potentially including elements within the CIA, the Mafia, or the military-industrial complex, to orchestrate Kennedy's assassination. This theory often involves claims that Oswald's actions were manipulated, evidence was fabricated, or that other shooters were involved. The theory's origins trace back to the immediate aftermath of the assassination, fueled by perceived inconsistencies in the official Warren Commission report and Oswald's ambiguous background. Key evidence cited includes alleged discrepancies in the ballistic evidence, conflicting witness testimonies, Oswald's prior association with government agencies, and the "magic bullet" theory. Mainstream scholarly perspective, based on extensive investigations including the Warren Commission and subsequent analyses, generally concludes that Oswald acted alone, though some acknowledge potential shortcomings in the initial investigation. Critics point to the lack of conclusive evidence supporting a conspiracy, the implausibility of maintaining such a complex operation's secrecy over decades, and the tendency of conspiracy theories to rely on selective interpretation of evidence and speculation.
subtitle: Oswald as scapegoat in JFK assassination

claims.claim: Oswald was not a lone gunman.
claims.evidence: Witness testimonies suggesting multiple shooters; ballistic evidence discrepancies.
claims.criticism: Conflicting testimonies often unreliable; ballistic evidence largely supports Oswald as the shooter.

claims.claim: Oswald was a government agent or informant.
claims.evidence: Oswald's past connection to the military and possible ties to intelligence agencies.
claims.criticism: Evidence of government connections is circumstantial and does not prove involvement in the assassination.

claims.claim: Evidence was fabricated or manipulated to frame Oswald.
claims.evidence: Alleged alterations to the Zapruder film; inconsistencies in the chain of custody of key evidence.
claims.criticism: Allegations of evidence tampering largely unsubstantiated; Zapruder film's authenticity generally accepted.

keyFigures.name: Mark Lane
keyFigures.role: Author of 'Rush to Judgment', a key early critique of the Warren Commission.

keyFigures.name: Jim Garrison
keyFigures.role: New Orleans District Attorney who pursued a conspiracy prosecution.

keyFigures.name: Gerald Posner
keyFigures.role: Author of 'Case Closed', a prominent defense of the lone gunman theory.

mythologyConnections: The idea of hidden puppet masters

timeline.event: John F. Kennedy assassinated in Dallas, Texas.
Lee Harvey Oswald shot and killed by Jack Ruby.
Warren Commission report concludes Oswald acted alone.
House Select Committee on Assassinations (HSCA) concludes there was a probable conspiracy.

sources.author: Warren Commission
sources.work: Report of the President's Commission on the Assassination of President Kennedy
sources.title: Warren Commission

sources.author: Jim Garrison

sources.title: Jim Garrison
